'''Busurra''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] who joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] in the [[Atrivis sector/Legends|Atrivis sector]]. There, he was an able administrator and an assistant to the [[Sector/Legends|sector's]] [[Chief of Staff/Legends|Chief of Staff]], and on a number of occasions was himself acting Chief of Staff. Busurra was mild-mannered, despite being [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] by forces of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].

==Biography==
Busurra was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]]. He was once captured by forces of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] who [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] him, blinding him in his right [[Eye/Legends|eye]] and causing him to lose three fingers on his left hand. To cover his injury, Busurra wore an [[Eye patch/Legends|eye patch]]. After joining the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], Busurra went on to become an assistant to [[Loom Carplin]], the [[Chief of Staff/Legends|Chief of Staff]] of the [[Atrivis sector/Legends|Atrivis]] [[Atrivis Sector Force|Sector Force]]. The pair were based on [[Generis/Legends|Generis]], the headquarters of the [[Sector/Legends|sector]] forces; however, Carplin was often [[Planet/Legends|off-world]] checking numerous outposts in the sector. On these occasions, Busurra was acting Chief of Staff, aiding [[Commander-in-Chief/Legends|Commander-in-Chief]] [[Travia Chan/Legends|Travia Chan]].<ref name="RASB" />

==Personality and traits==
Despite the reputation of his [[Species/Legends|species]] and the mutilation delivered to him by the Imperials, Busurra was a good-natured and mild mannered Wookiee. He was a very efficient administrator and organizer, and was aided in this role by his [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]], [[C3-L1]], who translated his speech for the many [[Human/Legends|Humans]] who struggled to understand him.<ref name="RASB" />

==Skills and abilities==
Busurra was skilled in combat, trained to use both a standard [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] and a [[Bowcaster/Legends|bowcaster]], as well as larger blaster [[Artillery/Legends|artillery]] weapons. He was also skilled in [[Unarmed combat/Legends|unarmed combat]]. Busurra was knowledgeable about [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]], along with their cultures and languages, and about [[Star system/Legends|planetary systems]]. A capable administrator, Busurra was well versed in the inner workings of a [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucracy]]. He was trained to [[Astrogation/Legends|navigate]] [[Starship/Legends|starships]] through [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], and was capable of [[Pilot/Legends|flying]] space [[Transport/Legends|transports]] and driving [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] vehicles.<ref name="RASB" />

Busurra was trained to operate the weapon systems and [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]] aboard starships, and was skilled in repairing space transports. Busurra also had survival training, expertise in demolition operations, bypassing security systems, and applying first aid.<ref name="RASB" />

==Equipment==
Busurra owned a [[Bowcaster/Legends|bowcaster]], the traditional Wookiee weapon, with which he was proficient. He also carried a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], and owned C3-L1, a protocol droid.<ref name="RASB" />

==Behind the scenes==
Busurra was created for the [[1990]] ''Rebel Alliance Sourcebook'' published by [[West End Games]].<ref name="RASB" /> In the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'', Bursurra's name was used as one of the leaders for AI players using the Wookiee faction, appearing only in the standard game mode.<ref name="GB">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''</ref>
